Primary Needs for Home Health (check all that apply):
When we call, we will also need to obtain the following information before seeing the
patient. If you have these available, feel free to fax along with this form.
Recent H&P or recent progress notes
Perform at: Southern Hills Hospital & Medical Center
Diagnosis: Shoulder Arthritis, Left - M19.012, M75.22, M24.012
Procedure Description: left total shoulder arthroplasty
23430: tenodesis of long tendon of biceps
23472: arthroplasty, glenohumeral joint; total shoulder (glenoid and proximal humeral replacement (eg, total shoulder))
23333: removal of foreign body, shoulder; deep (subfascial or intramuscular)
HPI: This is an 81 year old male who:
is being seen for a chief complaint of left shoulder. Anthony presents today for left shoulder
He is scheduled for a left reverse total shoulder arthroplasty on 04/07/26 at Southern Hills
His pain is a 3 out of 10.
Left upper extremity exam notable for the
Active forward flexion to 135 degrees. 4 out
of 5 rotator cuff strength testing. Positive
Hawkins, Neer, and empty can test. Positive
mid arc pain test. Crepitus with range of
motion. Positive speeds and Yergason's Test.
Associated diagnoses: Bicipital Tendinitis and Loose Body, Shoulder
Patient Specific Notes: Anthony presents today for preoperative visit. He is scheduled for a
left reverse total shoulder on April 7th at Southern Hills Hospital. All questions regarding
surgery were answered. He was provided with post operative pain medication and a sling.
Aspirin therapy was discussed, he has this at home. He will follow up 2 weeks after
Please refer to the education handout for detailed counseling.
Initiate Treatment: Initiate treatment with prescription medication.
Based on Risk Selected: Low Risk (0-3 points)
Sig: Take 1 tablet orally every 6 hours as needed for pain.
Quantity: 28 Tablet Earliest fill date: March 28, 2026
Follow up for: Further Evaluation and Management - 5 minutes. Other
Bhatia, Sonu MD - Primary Care Provider (PCP)
